scale.usage: Landing hero headlines (hero-passwords "Your employees' passwords are already on the dark web.") and the largest KPI figures (88%, 440, 266, 256, 155) which render at this size in 700-800. ExtraBold. White on dark navy/black hero; near-black (#0F1222) on light. Accent phrases recolored inline (red/green).

Marketing section headings ("Your Company's Logins Are Already Moving Across the Dark Web", "See what's exposed…"). Bold. App/report page titles ("Identity theft protection", "eczacibasi.com.tr — Credential Exposure Report") use a compact 32-34px variant of this role at weight 700-800.
Sub-section headings ("Executive Summary", "Exposure by Infection Vector / Feed Source", "Critical: Corporate Email Credentials"). Bold, near-black or white.
Panel/section titles ("Employee exposure"), feature card headings. SemiBold, #0F1222 / white.
Nav tabs (active tab 600 in brand purple #6D28D9), smaller card titles, emphasized stat/metric labels. SemiBold.
Hero sub-copy and lead paragraphs ("One scan shows every company login already leaked on the dark web…"). Regular; muted lavender/gray on dark, #4B5563 on light.
Default paragraph text, table primary cells (person names use 500), form/stat labels ("Total records"). Regular 400; secondary descriptions in #6B7280.
Secondary/helper text, table meta and email rows (faheem@purevpn.com), section subtitles ("Details on employee exposure including identified risks…"). Regular, muted gray.
Footnotes, timestamps, and disclaimers ("Figures shown are from an anonymized real scan", "Report generated / Records analyzed" metadata). 400, or 500 for inline metadata keys.

ALL-CAPS tracked eyebrows and column headers. Eyebrows in brand purple ("DARK WEB EXPOSURE ASSESSMENT FOR BUSINESS") or red ("CONFIDENTIAL — INTERNAL SECURITY USE ONLY"). Table column headers ("TYPE", "SOURCE", "SOURCE TYPE", "EMAIL", "APPLICATION") ~13px at 600 in muted gray. Uppercase + positive tracking is the defining trait.
CTA button labels ("Scan Company Domain →", "Free Check", "Get Report"). SemiBold, sentence case (not uppercase), on green/purple filled buttons; white text.

notes:
Measurement method: cap-heights and x-heights sampled per screen, then halved (exports are ~2x). Calibrated against Plus Jakarta Sans metrics (cap 0.73em, x-height 0.536em). hero-passwords is 3582x1352 (~1791 CSS), home-landing/report 2880w (~1440 CSS), dashboard 3200x2000 (~1600 CSS).

Weight mapping (Plus Jakarta 200-800): ExtraBold 800 = hero display headlines and the big KPI numbers (256, 88%, 440) — the heaviest, most compressed type in the system. Bold 700 = all marketing/report section headings (h1/h2) and the tracked all-caps overlines/table headers. SemiBold 600 = panel titles (h3/h4), nav tabs, card titles, and buttons. Medium 500 = table primary cells (names), nav items, chips/badges, and light emphasis in body. Regular 400 = all paragraph, secondary, caption, and helper text. ExtraLight/Light (200/300) and italics were NOT observed in these four screens — reserve them for optional fine print / quotes only.

All-caps + letter-spacing: only two things are uppercase — the colored eyebrow labels (purple on marketing/hero, red on the report's CONFIDENTIAL banner) and table column headers. Both carry positive tracking (~+0.06 to +0.10em). Everything else is sentence case, including buttons.

Letter-spacing philosophy: tighten as size grows (display/h1 ~-0.02em, h2/h3 ~-0.01em), neutral at body, and open up (+tracking) only for the small uppercase labels.

Color context: marketing/hero + report run on dark backgrounds (white headings, muted lavender/gray body, red & green accent words); the app dashboard is light (near-black #0F1222 headings, #6B7280 secondary, brand purple #6D28D9 for active/interactive, red #DC2626 for critical figures like Infected machines).
